title: hura polyandra
intro: Hura polyandra, also known as the mountain bread tree or breadnut tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant is known for its edible seeds, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The seeds of the hura polyandra plant have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, and have also been used to promote wound healing and improve digestion. Hura polyandra seeds can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be consumed raw or roasted as a nutritious food source.
